name: Bishop Museum
tagline: Hawaii's biggest museum with a world-class planetarium and a hands-on science center that rivals the mainland.
parking: Free parking on-site in museum lot
bestTime: Arrive at opening on a weekday; planetarium shows sell out so grab tickets first
kamaaina: Kamaaina rate with Hawaii ID; membership pays for itself in 2-3 visits
talkStory: Founded in 1889 by Charles Reed Bishop in honor of his wife Princess Bernice Pauahi Bishop, this is the largest museum in Polynesia and houses over 24 million artifacts including the world's foremost collection of Hawaiian and Pacific Island natural history. The Science Adventure Center's erupting volcano model was designed by the same firm behind Smithsonian exhibits. Most visitors miss the native Hawaiian garden behind the main hall and the original koa-wood Hawaiian Hall ceilings.
